intro: The biggest practical difference between Zipy and Hotjar is lock-in. Zipy is rated medium lock-in (session replay data exportable), while Hotjar is rated low lock-in (low — simple snippet, easy to replace). That changes how hard a future migration would be and how much strategy to put into exit planning. Beyond the headline, Zipy carries medium lock-in (session replay data exportable) and subscription from $0/month, with a free tier; Hotjar carries low lock-in (low — simple snippet, easy to replace) and subscription pricing, with a free tier. Zipy is a fit when you are ux debugging and optimization. Hotjar is a fit when you are ux debugging and optimization. The honest trade-off: Zipy is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare); Hotjar is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare). On the product side, Zipy is described as AI-powered session replay and error monitoring platform correlating frontend bugs with user session recordings, and Hotjar is described as UX analytics platform combining heatmaps, session recordings, surveys, and funnel analysis for web products.
quick_take: Zipy is for ux debugging and optimization; Hotjar is for ux debugging and optimization; decide on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Zipy if its medium lock-in is acceptable for your use case and you are ux debugging and optimization.
verdict_b: Choose Hotjar if minimizing lock-in matters and you accept its stated trade-offs for ux debugging and optimization.

faq.a: Both tools use subscription pricing. Zipy is listed as subscription from $0/month, with a free tier; Hotjar as subscription pricing, with a free tier. Without specific volume and seat assumptions it is not possible to say which is cheaper in general.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Zipy to Hotjar?
faq.a: Migration from Zipy to Hotjar is feasible but not trivial: Zipy has medium lock-in (session replay data exportable). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

Both tools tie at 4/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Hotjar a good alternative to Zipy?
faq.a: Hotjar targets the same best-fit audience as Zipy (ux debugging and optimization). Note that Hotjar is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare), so it only replaces Zipy if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (medium vs low), which is the main thing to weigh.
